lesson.title: 8 DRAWING AND PAINTING FROM OBSERVATION
topics: Drawing and painting from observation is a fundamental skill for artists, allowing them to
accurately capture the world around them.
This topic explores the techniques and principles of drawing and painting from observation,
helping artists refine their skills and create more realistic and engaging works. By observing and
activities:  Indoor (Art room with good lighting, easels…)
 Outdoor (Access to gardens, parks, or urban settings for live observation…)
 Technological (Use of tablets for digital drawing/painting applications…)
Drawing/ Painting (bond paper, sugar paper, black paper…) and sketchbooks
assessment:  Evaluate leaners’ artworks based on accuracy, creativity, and the application of
 Provide constructive feedback during peer reviews.
This topic exposed learners to acquire skills in drawing from observation and represent
their surroundings through drawings and paintings, fostering artistic skills and
appreciation of the visual world of Natural and Man-made objects.
